_comment: Opener-variety taxonomy — the closed set of sentence-opener CLASSES the openers pass measures. One letter per class; classify.mjs tags every sentence with exactly one. The instrument's whole point: distinguish bare subject-led (S — the AI monoculture) from the varied species. Heuristic, not a parser — see classify.mjs. Budgets are per-CHAPTER soft ceilings that gate a chapter into the review queue; they are not targets to hit mechanically (over-varying is its own tell).
_onVoice_comment: AUTHOR RULE (June 2026, craft/sentence/variety.md Operating Rule F): NO opener style is off-voice. The bare subject-led entrance (S) is the manuscript's overused default — use it MORE SPARINGLY. The varied styles P/C/J/A/G/I should be MORE PROMINENT in the mix; reach for whichever is the best writing. (An earlier draft banned dependent-clause/participial openers as 'off-voice' — that was an AI-inherited constraint from the retired VOICE.md, removed by author direction.) `prefer:'reduce'` = lean on it less; `prefer:'raise'` = bring it forward; the JUDGE may recast toward any varied style.

S.label: subject-led
S.blurb: bare [subject] [verb] — pronoun/proper-noun/article + verb. The overused default; lean on it noticeably less (still the music in a genuine signature run — keep those).

P.label: prepositional
P.blurb: opens on a prepositional / time-place phrase. Bring forward — great for scene-setting.
P.example: In the shadow of the mill, they waited.

C.label: subordinate
C.blurb: opens on a dependent clause (When/Because/As/If…). Fully sanctioned — bring forward where it's the best writing.
C.example: When the bell rang, the room stilled.

J.label: conjunction-led
J.blurb: opens on a coordinating conjunction (And/But/Yet/So). Plain and effective; bring forward.

A.label: adverbial
A.blurb: opens on an adverb or temporal/sequence word. Bring forward.

G.label: participial
G.blurb: opens on a participial / gerund phrase. Fully sanctioned — bring forward (watch for dangling modifiers: the participle must attach to the main-clause subject).
G.example: Clutching the letter, she stepped into the fray.

I.label: inversion
I.blurb: inverted or existential order (There/Here + verb; fronted complement). Bring forward sparingly.

D.label: dialogue
D.blurb: spoken line or a question/exclamation. Parked — voice, not narration; brogue's turf.

F.label: fragment
F.blurb: intentional fragment, incl. the negation-correction signature beat. /tic owns these — never double-thin.

budgets._comment: Per-chapter soft ceilings. A chapter enters the queue only if it trips one. Tuned from a first whole-book measure pass — adjust in tally.mjs after reading the report.
budgets._provenance: typeRun_min=4 is now an AUTHOR rule (craft/sentence/variety.md Operating Rule F): four sentences in a row opening on the SAME style — any style — is a flag to raise. pctS_max is lowered to reflect 'use subject-led more sparingly' (the book currently sits ~60% S; this target leans it down). Both are tunable; the real test stays human (flat/errand → vary; genuine signature run → keep).
budgets._typeRun_note: 4+ sentences in a row sharing ONE code is the flag, applied UNIFORMLY to every narrative style (S, P, C, J, A, G, I) — a P-wall is as much a monoculture as an S-wall. Dialogue (D) and fragment (F) runs are parked (owned by brogue / tic).

reconcile._comment: Overlap with the /variety pass (same first WORD 3x). Openers owns structural same-TYPE monotony; /variety owns literal same-WORD pile-ups. A run that is BOTH gets annotated alsoVariety=true here — never emitted twice.

targets._comment: AUTHOR target distribution (June 2026). Whole-book opener mix to steer the manuscript toward — bands, not exact quotas (over-fitting a target is its own monoculture). The book currently sits ~60.6% S / ~6.8% varied; these bands lean it down to 40–55% S with the varied species 15–30%. The UI reads this to show each chapter's mix vs. target. min/max are fractions of all sentences in scope.

S.note: subject-led — down from 60.6%; keeps clarity and momentum.

F.note: fragments — 21% is fine; cap it to maintain punch (/tic owns).

D.note: dialogue — depends on the scene; strong as-is.

varied.note: P+G+A+C+J+I combined — boost significantly. Prepositional & subordinate shine for setting/immersion; participial for action.
